intro: Real conditional sentences describe situations that are possible or realistic. In Spanish, many real conditionals use si with the indicative, followed by another indicative verb.

overview.body: Use this structure when the condition can really happen. The si clause gives the condition, and the main clause gives the result.

examples.english: If I have time, I study Spanish.

examples.spanish: Si llueve, nos quedamos en casa.
examples.english: If it rains, we stay at home.

structure.translation: If I am hungry, I eat something.

structure.example: Voy al parque si hace sol.
structure.translation: I go to the park if it is sunny.

structure.pattern: si + present indicative, future or command-like result
structure.translation: If you need help, I will call you.

uses.heading: Real possibilities
uses.body: Use the indicative when the condition is possible, factual, or likely.

examples.spanish: Si Ana está en casa, la visitamos.
examples.english: If Ana is at home, we visit her.
If you can, we start now.

uses.heading: General truths and routines
uses.body: Real conditionals can describe what normally happens when a condition is true.

examples.english: If I work a lot, I get tired.

examples.spanish: Si los niños tienen hambre, comen fruta.
examples.english: If the children are hungry, they eat fruit.

affirmative.english: If I study, I learn more.

affirmative.spanish: Si tienes café, tomo una taza.
affirmative.english: If you have coffee, I drink a cup.

affirmative.spanish: Vamos al cine si termina temprano.
affirmative.english: We go to the movies if it ends early.

affirmative.spanish: Si hace frío, cierro la ventana.
affirmative.english: If it is cold, I close the window.

negative.spanish: Si no tengo dinero, no compro nada.
negative.english: If I do not have money, I do not buy anything.
We do not go out if it rains.

negative.spanish: Si él no llama, no esperamos.
negative.english: If he does not call, we do not wait.

negative.spanish: Si no entiendes, no sigues adelante.
negative.english: If you do not understand, you do not continue.

questions.english: What do you do if you have time?
Do you come if I invite you?
If it rains, do we cancel the class?
Do you call me if you need help?

select.answer: Si llueve, nos quedamos en casa.
Voy al parque si hace sol.
Si tienes café, tomo una taza.
Si hace frío, cierro la ventana.

complete.answer: Si tienes tiempo, ven a casa.

complete.prompt: Si no tengo dinero, no ___ nada.
complete.answer: Si no tengo dinero, no compro nada.
Si Ana está aquí, la saludamos.

If it rains, we stay at home.

translate.prompt: We do not go out if it rains.
If he calls, I listen to him.
If it is cold, I close the window.
translate.answer: Si hace frío, cierro la ventana.

translate.prompt: Call me if you need help.
What do you do if you are free?

wrap_up.translation: If I study, I learn more.

wrap_up.example: Voy al parque si hace sol.
wrap_up.translation: I go to the park if it is sunny.
If it rains, we do not go out.
